Meta.title: Leandro Gómez Cano — one startup per month
Meta.description: Regional Director of Operations at tizo. I ship one product idea per month until year-end and document the process in public: startups, operations, growth, AI, and execution.
Meta.ogDescription: One new product each month until December. Public documentation of process, decisions, and metrics. Operations, growth, and applied AI.
Meta.twitterDescription: Build in public: monthly launches, learnings, and outcomes. Startup operator and product builder.

Hero.footnote: Living doc · decisions and metrics when they add context

About.title: Operations and product on the same plane
About.lead: I connect strategy with execution: I design systems that turn priorities into measurable delivery.
About.body: I'm Leandro Gómez Cano, Regional Director of Operations at tizo. I work with startups and high-tempo teams on operations, growth, artificial intelligence, and product. My focus isn't talk—it's the system that sustains growth.

blocks.label: What it is
blocks.text: A commitment through December: each month a new product idea—from problem definition to launch and a minimum viable iteration.

blocks.label: Why
blocks.text: Speed forces decisions. Documenting in the open enforces clarity, cuts self-deception, and accelerates learning against real markets.

blocks.label: What I'll build
blocks.text: Actionable prototypes and MVPs: tools, flows, and experiments that can be validated with users without empty narrative.

blocks.label: What I share
blocks.text: Scope decisions, trade-offs, metrics that matter, mistakes, and adjustments. No glamour: judgment and numbers when relevant.

blocks.label: What I want to learn
blocks.text: How to compress validation time, what to repeat in operations, and how to fold AI into the product cycle without losing business focus.

Classes.title: Hands-on training, beyond the monthly challenge
Classes.subtitle: Sessions focused on tools and operational judgment. They complement build in public; they do not replace it.
Classes.ctaPrimary: Get priority for the first cohort
Classes.ctaHint: Takes you to the footer block to leave your email when the list goes live.

items.title: AI chatbot for entrepreneurs
items.description: How to design and ship an AI chatbot aligned with your offer, data, and operations—not a generic demo. For founders and teams who need conversion and clarity, not just technology.

Roadmap.title: One launch per month · 2026
Roadmap.subtitle: Remaining months of the year. Status and learnings update each cycle.

items.description: A product for pet adoption: connect shelters and adopters with clear criteria, useful filters, and an application flow without unnecessary friction.
items.keyLearning: First cycle in progress: validate pet supply and adoption criteria before scaling features.

items.description: Month reserved for the next launch; scope is set after the previous cycle closes.

Learnings.title: Process log
Learnings.subtitle: Short notes, no posture. What matters is what changed the judgment.

notes.title: Publish the reasoning, not just the outcome
notes.body: When I documented why I killed a feature, conversations had more signal than when I only showed the demo.

notes.title: Too much scope in sprint two
notes.body: I tried to cover two segments at once. Learning diluted. I went back to one segment and one job-to-be-done.

notes.title: Measure before polishing
notes.body: I'd block time for metric definition on kickoff day—before UI design.

notes.title: Shipping faster exposes the real bottleneck
notes.body: Friction wasn't in the code—it was in the offer and channel. Early launch made that obvious.

notes.title: Ad-hoc playbooks don't scale
notes.body: I moved from loose notes to a repeatable checklist for pilot onboarding. Less chaos, more signal.

Thesis.title: Beliefs I bet on
lines: AI changes how products are designed and shipped.
Latin America needs more operators who turn ideas into systems.
What you don't measure gets mistaken for progress.
Clarity is a competitive asset—you train it with explicit decisions.

FinalCta.title: If you care how decisions get made under pressure, this is the place.
FinalCta.body: I'll post launches, metrics when they add context, and lessons without executive varnish. Choose where to follow the challenge.
FinalCta.newsletterHint: Leave your email when I turn on sends; for now it's a visual placeholder.

Site.headline: One Startup per Month
Site.heroSub: Until year-end I launch one new product idea each month and document the process in public: decisions, learnings, metrics, and outcomes.
Site.purpose: My purpose is to strengthen Nicaragua's startup ecosystem and help founders ship their ideas.
